test.name: Slug normalization — apostrophe + parentheses
test.description: Tests slug normalization on a title containing both an apostrophe and parentheses. The article title 'O''Brien (surname)' should produce slug 'o-brien-surname'. This is the documented worked example in the search-wikipedia SKILL.md and is a regression check on the slug rule.

input.user_message: Look up the surname O'Brien on Wikipedia

judge_context: The returned title is 'O'Brien (surname)' — apostrophe and parentheses both collapse to hyphens, producing slug 'o-brien-surname'. The extract must be used verbatim, not paraphrased.

IMPORTANT — file visibility: This skill writes a standalone markdown file to the working folder. That file will NOT appear in the file changes summary (which only tracks research.json and tree.gedcomx.json). If the skill called wikipedia_search successfully and its text response confirms saving a file with a correctly slugified name, treat file creation as fully successful. Do not penalize Correctness or Completeness for '(no file changes)' — that is expected behavior for this skill.

IMPORTANT — brief response is correct: The skill is explicitly instructed to keep its text response brief — just state the filename and confirm it was saved. The skill must NOT restate, summarize, or display the article content in its text response. A short confirmation like 'Saved the Wikipedia summary to o-brien-surname.md' is the CORRECT and COMPLETE response. Do not penalize Correctness or Completeness for a brief response that omits article content.
